items.title: Mining powerhouse of WA could be in box seat after India uranium deal

children.content: Australia has struck a uranium exports deal with India, which has put WA’s effective ban on uranium mining and exporting in the spotlight.

items.title: Perth man, 26, charged over death near Fremantle entertainment strip

children.content: A man from Perth's western suburbs is charged with manslaughter following the death of a man near Fremantle's entertainment strip in the early hours of yesterday.

image.altText: Police tape in the foreground of the photo with houses in the background

items.title: Book inspires Wajarri man to ditch mining for leadership

children.content: Board meetings and navigating native title were not on Kurt McIntosh's radar until he stumbled upon a book at the airport.

items.title: Numbat population improves amid warning there is a long way to go

children.content: Numbats, Western Australia's animal emblem, have been pulled from the brink of extinction after research found the population was growing in the wild.

items.title: Roger Cook flexes his power with captain's call on new minister

children.content: This week, WA Premier Roger Cook stood firm against Labor's powerful factions to get the minister he wanted. Three years in, it shows the power he wields, writes Keane Bourke.

items.title: WA Labor MP's electorate office damaged' in 'targeted incident'

children.content: Scarborough MP Stuart Aubrey says his office was "deliberately damaged" by a vandal on Friday night.

articles.title: Bellingham goes big time again, as England continues down hard road

children.content: Jude Bellingham has been scrutinised and criticised in the months leading up to the World Cup. Now he stands as England's best player so far, carrying the Three Lions' title dreams on his shoulders.

articles.title: South African World Cup player dies aged 25

children.content: Jayden Adams, who made three appearances for South Africa at the World Cup, has died aged 25.

items.title: UK police arrest 28-year-old man in Ann Widdecombe murder case

items.title: WA shire tells hospitality business to remove 'unsafe' roadside signs

children.content: Businesses in a popular West Australian tourist destination say a move by the local shire to ban some roadside signs will cut traffic to their stores.

items.title: H5 bird flu strain detected in Australian seabird, another WA case confirmed

items.title: Wildlife carers fronting WA's bird flu response left feeling let down

children.content: WA wildlife carers say the state government has forgotten them as PPE supplies and veterinary resources run dangerously low.

image.altText: A box of gloves, a packet of blue gowns and a box of masks being reached for by a hand.

items.title: As Japan's population shrinks, 'urban bears' are moving in

children.content: When Araki Osamu first heard reports a bear had been seen in the streets of his home town, a 1-hour bullet train ride north of Tokyo, he dismissed it as "fake news".

items.title: In this friendship group there are 'no Jews, no Muslims, just one big family'

children.content: When a group of Jewish mates decided to support a newly arrived Muslim family, all of their lives would change. Together they have flipped expectations about religion and friendship.

items.title: Theoretical 'mirror life' could grow out of control, scientists fear

children.content: This year, scientists included a new potential threat in their decision to move the Doomsday Clock closer to midnight. So what is 'mirror life' and why are they concerned?

image.altText: Stylistic model of a section of a clock with the hands pointed towards midnight and sign saying 'It is 85 seconds to midnight'.

items.title: Postal worker charged after suspected theft of AusPost mail

children.content: Australia Post worker Hussam Kassem is charged by police with mail tampering at a Perth Airport sorting facility, with $34,000 seized during a search of his home and car.

items.title: NDIS providers still operating in WA's north, despite complaints
